scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a supported public-service status request only after case-management, eligibility, notice, service-ticket, and correspondence records aligned on a safe administrative summary.
Missing jurisdiction, program, case, service population, notice, identity, and channel facts produced a blocked-state request for prerequisites instead of guesswork.
A deadline bypass and enrollment workaround were rejected as unsupported policy exceptions and routed to the responsible adjudication or policy owner.
Eligibility and appeal-window risk triggered due-process-sensitive escalation rather than a substituted service-continuation decision.
A missing recertification filing, timestamp, receipt, and correspondence history were handled as an audit-trail blocker requiring records or program review.
Requests to order approval, sanction a provider, and confirm a penalty were refused and routed to program, enforcement, or legal owners.
FOIA, Privacy Act, civil-rights, and confidentiality conflicts were escalated to public records, privacy, civil-rights, or legal instead of being interpreted by the lane.
A procurement-sensitive contracted-provider complaint became a clean handoff to procurement, legal, or audit with solicitation, contract, and communication limits named.
Repeated missed callbacks, contradictory notices, and accommodation concerns were escalated as repeat-failure and equity-sensitive service issues with evidence history.
The lane preserved government terminology such as eligibility record, notice of action, service standard, public obligation, FOIA tracking number, procurement file, transparency log, and audit trail.
Conflicting tenant policy, service standard, program manual, and due-process notice context were recorded as a precedence conflict requiring escalation.
Tenant service-level, language-access, records-retention, and equity controls were applied without discarding the public baseline, and assumptions were documented.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, service-support-and-resolution, and government-and-public-sector abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and government-and-public-sector contract.

The package stays inside public-service delivery support and does not claim adjudication, enforcement, legal, procurement, public-records, privacy, audit, financial-approval, or delegated official authority.
